articles.headline: Fourth World Cup still has South Korea captain Son Heung-Min feeling 'like a kid'
articles.description: South Korea captain Son Heung-min says he feels like a "kid" as he enters his fourth World Cup, where he will debut against the Czech Republic in a Group A match in Guadalajara on Thursday.

images.name: Son Heung-Min [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min was all smiles on Wednesday at South Korea's final training session before their 2026 FIFA World Cup opener against Czechia.
images.credit: Ulises RUIZ / AFP via Getty Images

articles.headline: Will 2026 prove to be Son Heung-Min's final FIFA World Cup hurrah?
articles.description: Son Heung-Min's previous three World Cups perfectly mirrored where he was in his career. So what does 2026 hold for him?

images.name: Will the 2026 World Cup be Son Heung-Min's last hurrah? [1500x844]
Son Heung-Min 2014 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's first World Cup goal came in South Korea's ill-fated 2014 campaign -- where they picked up just one point from three games in their worst showing since 1998.
images.credit: ADRIAN DENNIS / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2018 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's memorable sealer in a stunning 2-0 win over Germany in 2018 ultimately proved in vain as South Korea narrowly failed to make it out of the group stage.

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2022 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: South Korea finally reached the round of 16 at the 2022 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 12 years -- although that was where their campaign would end with a 4-1 loss to Brazil.

images.name: Will Mexico or South Korea top Group A at the World Cup?
images.caption: Gab & Juls debate who will win Group A between Mexico, South Korea, Czechia and South Africa at the FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Japan have a plan to win the World Cup by 2050. How close will they get in 2026?
articles.description: Japan's "100 Year Vision" is well ahead of schedule, but are the Samurai Blue ready win the World Cup now?

images.name: Japan World Cup chances [1500x844]
images.alt: Wataru Endo, Takefusa Kubo and Ritsu Doan

images.name: Wataru Endo [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was sidelined by injury in February, Wataru Endo has recovered in time for this third FIFA World Cup -- and first as captain of Japan.

images.name: Japan vs. Brazil [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup saw them claim friendly victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.name: Pochettino: USMNT are moving in a good direction ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Midfield metronome Hwang In-Beom could be key for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Hwang In-Beom is not South Korea's most-prominent name. But he could be their most important player at the World Cup.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was initially a doubt after picking up an injury in March, Hwang In-Beom has been passed fit to feature for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup -- where he will likely play a key role for them in midfield.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Having had plenty of previous experience in Europe with Rubin Kazan, Olympiacos and Red Star Belgrade, Hwang In-Beom has now spent the past two seasons in the Eredivisie with Feyenoord.

images.name: Son Heung-min set for fourth World Cup with South Korea
images.caption: South Korea name their 26-man squad for the 2026 World Cup, headlined by a fourth competition appearance for captain Son Heung-min.

articles.headline: Why Japan could be the most exciting team at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Even with a couple of key players out through injury, Japan could still be the most exciting team at the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Japan [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup has seen them record victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever over the past seven months.

images.name: Kaoru Mitoma [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan were dealt a real blow ahead of the World Cup after injuy ruled out Kaoru Mitoma, who scored the only goal of the game when they beat England 1-0 at Wembley back in March.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

season.name: 2026 World Cup Qualifying - AFC, Third Round

article.headline: South Korea qualify for 2026 World Cup with 2-0 win over 10-man Iraq
article.description: VIDEO SHOWS: HIGHLIGHTS FROM SOUTH KOREA 2-0 WIN OVER IRAQ TO QUALIFY FOR WORLD CUP
article.linkText: South Korea qualify for 2026 World Cup with 2-0 win over 10-man Iraq
article.story: VIDEO SHOWS: HIGHLIGHTS FROM SOUTH KOREA 2-0 WIN OVER IRAQ TO QUALIFY FOR WORLD CUP (RESENDING WITH SHOTLIST) SHOWS: BASRA, IRAQ (JUNE 5, 2025)(AFC - See restrictions) SOUTK KOREA (RED JERSEYS) AT IRAQ AT BASRA INTERNATIONAL STADIUM - WORLD CUP QUALIFYING MATCH 1. MATCH OFFICIALS AND PLAYERS WALKING ONTO FIELD BEFORE START 2. FANS IN STANDS WITH LARGE IRAQ FLAGS 3. PLAYERS WALKING ONTO FIELD 4. SOUTH KOREAN PLAYERS AND FLAG DURING ANTHEM 5. VARIOUS OF IRAQ FANS AND PLAYERS DURING ANTHEM 1ST HALF 6. SOUTH KOREA'S CHO YU-MIN IS KICKED IN THE FACE BY IRAQ'S ALI AL-HAMADI 7. SLOW MOTION REPLAY OF AL-HAMADI KICKING CHO YU-MIN IN FACE 8. REFEREE GIVING AL-HAMADI RED CARD IN 26TH MINUTE 2ND HALF 9. GOAL - SOUTH KOREA'S LEE KANG-IN WITH PASS TO KIM JIN-KYU WHO SCORES PAST IRAQ GOALKEEPER JALAL HASSAN IN 63RD MINUTE. SOUTH KOREA 1-0 IRAQ 10. REPLAYS OF LEE KANG-IN PASS TO KIM JIN-KYU FOR GOAL 11. GOAL - SOUTH KOREA'S SE-JIN JEON WITH CENTERING PASS TO OH HYEON-GU WHO SCORES PAST IRAQ'S HASSAN IN 82ND MINUTE. SOUTH KOREA 2-0 IRAQ 12. REPLAYS SE-JIN JEON CENTERING PASS TO OH HYEON-GU FOR GOAL 13. SOUTH KOREA'S OH HYEON-GU HELPING IRAQ PLAYER UP JUST AFTER REFEREE BLOWS WHISTLE TO END MATCH. SOUTH KOREA WIN 2-0 14. IRAQ'S MOHAMMED QASIM MAJID AFTER LOSS 15. IRAQ MANAGER GRAHAM ARNOLD GREETING SOUTH KOREA MANAGER HONG MYUNG-BO AFTER MATCH 16. SOUTH KOREAN PLAYERS AFTER WIN STORY: Regular qualifiers South Korea booked a spot Thursday (June 5) in the 2026 North American World Cup with a 2-0 win over Iraq in Basra. Kim Jin-gyu and Oh Hyeon-gyu were on target in the second half for the Koreans against 10-man Iraq and that win not only secured qualification for Hong Myung-bo's side from Group B but confirmed Jordan would also progress. In other Asian action, Uzbekistan qualified for the first time in the expanded 48-team finals with a 0-0 draw against the United Arab Emirates, which guaranteed Timur Kapadze's side a top two finish in Group A of the preliminaries alongside already-qualified Iran. The west Asians had beaten Oman 3-0 earlier in the evening through a hat-trick from Ali Olwan, but Jamal Sellami's side had to wait for the outcome of Iraq's meeting with the Koreans before being able to celebrate their World Cup berth. Australia also stand on the cusp of a place at the finals after Aziz Behich scored the only goal in his side's 1-0 win over Japan in Perth in Group C. Victory keeps Tony Popovic's side three points clear of Saudi Arabia, who won 2-0 against Bahrain in Riffa, with one game to play. Goals from Musab Al-Juwayr and Abdulrahman Al-Obud earned Saudi Arabia a win that put Australia's celebrations on hold until the two nations meet in Jeddah on Tuesday. But Australia's vastly superior goal difference means they can lose that meeting with the Saudis by up to four goals and still advance to a sixth consecutive World Cup finals as second-placed finishers behind the Japanese. Uzbekistan goalkeeper Utkir Yusupov made a string of second-half saves in Abu Dhabi to frustrate the UAE and earn his nation their first World Cup qualification since the country gained independence from the Soviet Union in 1991. The draw means the UAE will now progress to the fourth round of qualifying as the point earned guarantees a third- or fourth-placed finish in Group A. They will be joined in the next phase by 2022 World Cup hosts Qatar, who managed a 1-0 win over 10-man Iran through Pedro Correia's 41st-minute close-range goal. Indonesia guaranteed their involvement would also continue into the fourth round with a 1-0 win over China that ended the qualification hopes of Branko Ivankovic's side. Ole Romeny's 45th-minute penalty separated the sides in Jakarta to ensure Indonesia would finish in either third or fourth place in Group C, thereby progressing to the next phase. Goals from Tamer Seyam and Wessam Abou Ali kept Palestine's chances of going through to the next round intact, with a 2-0 win over hosts Kuwait moving Ihab Abujazar's side to within one point of Oman in fourth place in Group B. The first two finishers in each of the three qualifying groups progress automatically to the World Cup while the teams in third and fourth advance to October's fourth round to battle for another two berths at the finals. Kyrgyzstan will not be among those competing for the remaining slots after their 2-2 draw with North Korea in Riyadh ended their chances of going through to the fourth round. (Production: David Grip)
